_metadata.description: AVD Assessor check definitions — CAF/WAF/LZA/SEC/FSL best practices
_metadata.weightScale: 1 = informational, 2 = recommended, 3 = important (default), 4 = strongly recommended, 5 = critical
_metadata.effortScale: Quick Win = config/toggle, Some Effort = planning/testing/downtime, Major Effort = infra redesign

checks.name: Entra ID Join Type
checks.description: Session hosts should use Entra ID Join or Hybrid Entra ID Join. Entra ID Join is preferred for cloud-native deployments; Hybrid Join is needed when on-prem AD resources (GPO, NTLM, Kerberos) are required

checks.name: MFA Enforcement
checks.description: Multi-factor authentication must be enforced for all AVD users via Conditional Access policies. This prevents credential-based attacks such as password spray and phishing from granting session access

checks.name: Conditional Access Policies
checks.description: Conditional Access policies should target the Azure Virtual Desktop and Microsoft Remote Desktop cloud apps to control sign-in frequency, device compliance, location restrictions, and session persistence

checks.name: AVD RBAC Roles
checks.description: Use built-in AVD RBAC roles (Desktop Virtualization User, Contributor, Reader, etc.) instead of broad roles like Owner or Contributor. This limits blast radius if a service principal or admin account is compromised

checks.name: Least-Privilege Access Model
checks.description: Administrative access across the AVD environment should follow least-privilege principles — including PIM for just-in-time elevation, restricted local admin on session hosts, and scoped service principal permissions

checks.name: Break-Glass Accounts
checks.description: At least two emergency (break-glass) accounts should be configured with permanent Global Admin rights, excluded from Conditional Access, and stored securely. These prevent lockout during authentication outages

checks.name: Hub-Spoke Topology
checks.description: AVD networking should follow a hub-spoke topology where shared services (DNS, firewall, ExpressRoute/VPN gateways) reside in the hub, and AVD session hosts are deployed in dedicated spoke VNets with peering

checks.name: NSG on AVD Subnets
checks.description: Network Security Groups should be applied to all AVD subnets to control inbound/outbound traffic. At minimum, deny inbound RDP from internet, allow outbound to AVD service tags, and restrict inter-subnet flows

checks.name: No Public IP on Session Hosts
checks.description: Session hosts must not have public IP addresses. Users connect through the AVD reverse-connect gateway, which eliminates the need for direct inbound connectivity and prevents internet-facing attack surface
checks.impact: Public IPs on session hosts expose them directly to the internet, bypassing network-level protection and enabling direct brute-force attacks.

checks.name: RDP Port 3389 Not Exposed
checks.description: Inbound RDP (port 3389) must not be exposed to the internet via NSG rules. AVD uses reverse-connect transport, so no inbound ports are needed. Open RDP is the #1 brute-force target in Azure
checks.impact: Exposed RDP port 3389 is the #1 brute-force target. Attackers scan Azure IP ranges continuously for open RDP.

checks.name: Private Link / Private Endpoints
checks.description: Azure Private Link should be configured for the AVD control plane (feed discovery, broker, gateway) to keep management traffic on the Microsoft backbone and prevent control-plane data from traversing the public internet

checks.name: Azure Firewall / NVA
checks.description: All outbound traffic from AVD subnets should route through Azure Firewall or a third-party NVA for URL filtering, threat intelligence, FQDN-based rules, and logging of egress traffic patterns

checks.name: RDP Shortpath Managed Networks
checks.description: RDP Shortpath for managed networks enables direct UDP transport between client and session host over VPN/ExpressRoute, reducing latency and improving media quality by bypassing the AVD gateway relay

checks.name: Network Latency Requirements
checks.description: Round-trip latency from end-user locations to the Azure region hosting AVD should be validated at <150ms for acceptable experience. Use the AVD Experience Estimator or Azure Connection Tool for measurement

checks.name: Accelerated Networking
checks.description: Accelerated Networking (SR-IOV) should be enabled on all session host NICs for lower latency, reduced jitter, and decreased CPU utilization. Most D-series and larger VM SKUs support this at no extra cost

checks.name: Custom DNS Configuration
checks.description: VNets hosting AD-joined session hosts must have custom DNS servers pointing to domain controllers for domain join, GPO processing, and Kerberos authentication. Azure-provided DNS will not resolve AD domains

checks.name: RDP Shortpath Public Networks
checks.description: RDP Shortpath for public networks uses STUN/TURN with UDP to establish direct transport for internet-connected clients, significantly improving latency, video quality, and Teams call performance

checks.name: Trusted Launch Enabled
checks.description: Session hosts should use Trusted Launch with Secure Boot and vTPM enabled. This provides hardware-rooted boot integrity verification, protects against bootkits/rootkits, and is required for Guest Attestation
checks.impact: Without Trusted Launch, VMs lack hardware-rooted security — no Secure Boot, no vTPM, no boot integrity attestation.
checks.remediation: Redeploy session hosts with -SecurityType TrustedLaunch (cannot be changed in-place).

checks.name: Max Session Limit Set
checks.description: Pooled host pools must have an explicit max session limit set based on workload testing. The default (999999) can overload a single host, causing poor performance, application crashes, and cascading failures
checks.impact: Unlimited session limit (999999) can overload a single host, causing poor user experience and potential crashes.

checks.name: VM Sizing Validation
checks.description: VM SKUs should be validated against Microsoft's workload profiles: light (2 vCPU/8GB), medium (4 vCPU/16GB), heavy/power (8+ vCPU/32GB+). Undersizing causes poor UX; oversizing wastes budget

checks.name: Golden Image Management
checks.description: Golden images should be built via automated pipelines (Azure Image Builder, Packer, or DevOps) with version tracking in Azure Compute Gallery. Manual image builds create drift and are not reproducible

checks.name: Patch Management Strategy
checks.description: A patch management strategy using Intune, Azure Update Manager, or WSUS should be in place with defined maintenance windows, compliance reporting, and rollback procedures for security updates

checks.name: Endpoint Protection
checks.description: Microsoft Defender Antivirus must be configured with VDI-specific exclusions (FSLogix VHD paths, Citrix paths if applicable) and real-time protection. Standard AV scans on VDI cause significant I/O storms
checks.impact: Session hosts without MDE lack real-time threat detection, leaving the multi-user environment vulnerable to malware spread.

checks.name: Screen Capture Protection
checks.description: Screen capture protection prevents screenshot and screen recording tools from capturing remote session content. This appears as a black screen when using Snipping Tool, OBS, or similar on the client side
checks.impact: Without screen capture protection, users can screenshot sensitive data displayed in remote sessions using local tools.
checks.remediation: Set RDP property: excludedprocesses:s: and screen capture protection in host pool custom RDP settings.

checks.name: Idle Timeout Policies
checks.description: Disconnect and logoff timeout policies should be configured to free resources from idle sessions. Recommended: disconnect after 15-30 min idle, logoff disconnected sessions after 1-6 hours

checks.name: Local Admin Restricted
checks.description: End users must not have local administrator rights on multi-session hosts. Local admin access enables software installation, security policy bypass, and potential lateral movement across all sessions on that host

checks.name: Availability Zones
checks.description: Session hosts should be spread across multiple availability zones (AZ1, AZ2, AZ3) for zone-level resilience. A single-zone deployment risks total loss of capacity during zone-level failures

checks.name: RDP Properties Security
checks.description: Custom RDP properties should explicitly restrict device redirection (drives, clipboard, USB, printers, cameras) based on security requirements. Default settings allow most redirections, increasing data exfiltration risk

checks.name: Managed Disk Encryption
checks.description: OS disks should use server-side encryption with either platform-managed keys (default) or customer-managed keys (CMK) for regulatory compliance. Unmanaged or unencrypted disks expose data at rest

checks.name: FSLogix Installed
checks.description: FSLogix agent must be installed on all multi-session hosts to provide profile container and Office container functionality. Without FSLogix, user profiles will be local-only and lost on pooled host logoff

checks.name: Profile Storage Private Endpoint
checks.description: Profile storage (Azure Files or Azure NetApp Files) should use private endpoints to keep SMB traffic on the Azure backbone. Public endpoint access exposes profile VHD/VHDX data to potential interception
checks.impact: Without private endpoints, profile traffic traverses the public internet, exposing user data and credentials in transit.

checks.name: Premium Storage for Profiles
checks.description: Premium tier Azure Files or Azure NetApp Files should be used for FSLogix profile containers. Standard tier may not provide sufficient IOPS during login storms when many users authenticate simultaneously

checks.name: HTTPS Only on Storage
checks.description: Profile storage accounts must enforce HTTPS-only to prevent plaintext transmission of user profile data, cached credentials, and Outlook OST files over unencrypted HTTP connections
checks.impact: Allowing HTTP enables plaintext data transfer of user profile data, credentials, and session information.

checks.name: Minimum TLS 1.2
checks.description: Profile storage must enforce TLS 1.2 minimum. TLS 1.0/1.1 have known vulnerabilities (POODLE, BEAST) that enable practical man-in-the-middle attacks to intercept FSLogix profile mount traffic
checks.impact: TLS versions below 1.2 have known vulnerabilities (POODLE, BEAST) that can be exploited to intercept profile data.

checks.name: Redirections.xml Optimized
checks.description: Redirections.xml should exclude high-churn, non-essential folders (Teams cache, browser cache, Downloads) from the profile container to reduce VHD size, improve logon times, and decrease storage costs

checks.name: OneDrive KFM Enabled
checks.description: OneDrive Known Folder Move redirects Desktop, Documents, and Pictures to OneDrive, reducing profile container size by keeping large files in cloud sync rather than inside the FSLogix VHD/VHDX

checks.name: Teams Optimization
checks.description: New Microsoft Teams (Teams 2.0) with AVD media optimization or MSIX-based deployment should be configured for optimal call quality, screen sharing, and background effect performance in remote sessions

checks.name: Diagnostic Settings Coverage
checks.description: All AVD resource types (host pools, workspaces, application groups) must have diagnostic settings forwarding checkpoint, error, management, connection, and host registration logs to a Log Analytics workspace for troubleshooting, alerting, and AVD Insights dashboard population
checks.impact: Without diagnostic settings, connection failures, session errors, and performance issues leave no audit trail for troubleshooting.

checks.name: AVD Insights Enabled
checks.description: AVD Insights workbook should be configured with a Log Analytics workspace receiving host pool diagnostics and session host performance data. This provides at-a-glance visibility into connection quality and user experience

checks.name: Service Health Alerts
checks.description: Azure Service Health alert rules should be configured for the Azure Virtual Desktop service to receive notifications about planned maintenance, service issues, and health advisories impacting the AVD control plane

checks.name: Azure Monitor Agent
checks.description: Azure Monitor Agent (AMA) should be installed on all session hosts to collect performance counters (CPU, memory, disk, network) and Windows event logs required for AVD Insights and custom alerting
checks.impact: Without Azure Monitor Agent, session hosts have no performance telemetry — CPU exhaustion, disk pressure, and memory leaks go undetected.

checks.name: Alerting Strategy
checks.description: Alert rules should be configured for key metrics: user input delay >200ms, round-trip time >150ms, available sessions <20%, and scaling plan failures — to enable proactive capacity and performance management

checks.name: Scaling Plan Assigned
checks.description: Scaling plans should be assigned and active on pooled host pools to automatically adjust session host count based on schedule and demand. This ensures cost optimization during off-peak and capacity during peak hours
checks.impact: Without a scaling plan, there is no automatic capacity adjustment — leading to over-provisioning (cost) or under-provisioning (outages).

checks.name: Storage Replication
checks.description: Profile storage should use ZRS (zone-resilient) or GRS (geo-redundant) replication instead of LRS. LRS provides no protection against zone-level failures, which can make all user profiles inaccessible

checks.name: RTO/RPO Targets Defined
checks.description: Recovery Time Objective (RTO) and Recovery Point Objective (RPO) should be defined, documented, and validated for the AVD service. These targets drive decisions about DR architecture, storage replication, and failover automation

checks.name: DR Testing Schedule
checks.description: Disaster recovery testing should follow a regular cadence (quarterly minimum) to validate that failover procedures work, DNS cutover completes within RTO, and user profiles are accessible from the secondary region

checks.name: Multi-Region Strategy
checks.description: A multi-region strategy (active-active, active-passive, or documented single-region acceptance) should be defined. This determines whether a secondary host pool, profile storage, and network infrastructure are maintained

checks.name: Image Replication
checks.description: Golden images should be replicated to secondary region(s) via Azure Compute Gallery replication. Without replicated images, DR host pool deployment is blocked until images are manually copied during an outage

checks.name: Start VM on Connect
checks.description: Start VM on Connect should be enabled to automatically start deallocated session hosts when a user connects, eliminating idle compute costs while maintaining instant availability. Works with scaling plans for full lifecycle management

checks.name: Resource Tags Applied
checks.description: All AVD resources should be tagged with at minimum: Environment, Owner, CostCenter, and Application. Tags enable cost allocation reports, automation targeting, and lifecycle management across resource groups
checks.impact: Untagged resources cannot be attributed to cost centers, owners, or environments — making governance and chargeback impossible.

checks.name: Validation Host Pool
checks.description: At least one host pool should be designated as a validation environment to receive AVD service updates before production. This provides early warning of breaking changes without impacting production users

checks.name: Cost Monitoring & Budgets
checks.description: Azure Cost Management budgets with action group alerts should be configured at the subscription or resource group level to detect and notify on unexpected spend from misconfigured scaling, orphaned resources, or overprovisioning

checks.name: Licensing Compliance
checks.description: Windows licensing must be validated — M365 E3/E5/A3/A5/F3/Business Premium or Windows E3/E5 per-user are required for multi-session. Per-user access pricing is required for external user access

checks.name: Naming Conventions
checks.description: Azure resource naming should follow documented organizational conventions (e.g., CAF naming guidance) for resource type, workload, environment, region, and instance — enabling identification at a glance

checks.name: App Group Configuration
checks.description: Application groups should be configured as Desktop for full desktop access or RemoteApp for individual published applications. Mixing both types requires separate host pools per the AVD architecture model

checks.name: MSIX/App Attach
checks.description: App Attach or MSIX App Attach decouples application lifecycle from the golden image, enabling admin-less app updates, version rollback, and per-user or per-group app targeting without reimaging hosts

checks.name: Printing Strategy
checks.description: Printing strategy should be defined — Universal Print for cloud-native, printer redirection for legacy, or a hybrid approach. Unrestricted printer redirection can cause driver conflicts and spooler crashes

checks.name: Enterprise Landing Zone
checks.description: An enterprise-scale landing zone should be deployed following the AVD Landing Zone Accelerator reference architecture, with platform services (connectivity, identity, management) separated from workload resources

checks.name: Resource Group Structure
checks.description: Resource groups should follow LZA patterns: separate groups for compute (session hosts), storage (profiles), networking (VNets, NSGs), and shared services (Key Vault, Log Analytics, galleries)

checks.name: Management Group Hierarchy
checks.description: Management group hierarchy should follow CAF guidance: Root > Platform (Connectivity, Identity, Management) > Landing Zones (Corp, Online) > Decommissioned/Sandbox. AVD workloads sit under the Corp landing zone

checks.name: Azure Policy Governance
checks.description: Azure Policy assignments at the management group or subscription level should enforce compliance baselines — required tags, allowed VM SKUs, required encryption, denied public IPs, and audit logging

checks.name: Subscription Segmentation
checks.description: Subscriptions should be segmented per CAF guidance: separate subscriptions for connectivity (hub VNet, firewall), identity (domain controllers), management (Log Analytics, Automation), and AVD workloads

checks.name: Application Control (WDAC/AppLocker)
checks.description: Windows Defender Application Control (WDAC) or AppLocker should restrict executable, script, and DLL execution to approved applications only. This prevents unauthorized software, malware, and shadow IT on multi-session hosts

checks.name: Watermarking Enabled
checks.description: QR code watermarking embeds user identity and connection info into the session display. This deters photography-based data theft and enables forensic tracing when screen photos leak sensitive information
checks.impact: Without watermarking, there is no deterrence against photographing sensitive data from remote sessions with personal devices.

checks.name: Credential Guard
checks.description: Credential Guard uses virtualization-based security to isolate LSA credentials in a protected container, preventing Pass-the-Hash and Pass-the-Ticket attacks from extracting credentials from session host memory

checks.name: VBS/HVCI Enabled
checks.description: Virtualization-Based Security (VBS) with Hypervisor-Protected Code Integrity (HVCI) prevents unsigned kernel-mode code from executing as drivers, blocking kernel rootkits and vulnerable driver exploits on session hosts

checks.name: Secure Score Review
checks.description: Microsoft Defender Secure Score recommendations specific to the AVD subscription should be reviewed quarterly. Each recommendation includes specific remediation steps to improve overall security posture scoring

checks.name: EDR Product Deployed
checks.description: Microsoft Defender for Endpoint (MDE) should be deployed on all session hosts for real-time threat detection, automated investigation, endpoint detection and response (EDR), and integration with Microsoft 365 Defender

checks.name: TVM Assessments Enabled
checks.description: Threat and Vulnerability Management (TVM) in Defender for Endpoint should be enabled to continuously assess session hosts for software vulnerabilities, misconfigurations, and missing security updates

checks.name: Screen Lock for Idle Sessions
checks.description: Screen lock policy should activate after a defined idle period (10-15 minutes recommended) requiring re-authentication. This prevents unauthorized physical access to unattended remote sessions displayed on client devices

checks.name: M365 Security Policy Advisor
checks.description: Security Policy Advisor in Microsoft 365 Apps admin center should be used to evaluate and apply recommended security policies for Office applications running within AVD sessions

checks.name: Global Secure Access (GSA)
checks.description: Global Secure Access (Entra Private Access, Internet Access, M365 Access) provides Zero Trust network access for session hosts, replacing traditional VPN with identity-aware, conditional access-integrated connectivity

checks.name: Confidential Computing Evaluated
checks.description: DCasv5/ECasv5 confidential computing VMs should be evaluated for workloads processing sensitive data. These VMs provide hardware-based encryption of data in use, protecting against host-level compromise

checks.name: Zero Trust Architecture
checks.description: Zero Trust principles (verify explicitly, least privilege, assume breach) should be applied across the AVD deployment including identity verification, device compliance, network segmentation, and data protection

checks.name: Dedicated OU in Active Directory
checks.description: AVD session hosts should be placed in a dedicated Active Directory OU to receive targeted GPOs for session timeout, drive mapping, printer redirection, and security baselines separate from general server policies

checks.name: Single Sign-On (SSO)
checks.description: Entra ID-based SSO eliminates the second credential prompt when connecting to session hosts. This improves UX and enables passwordless authentication flows within the remote session

checks.name: Passwordless Authentication
checks.description: Passwordless authentication methods (FIDO2 security keys, Windows Hello for Business, or passkeys) should be enabled for in-session use to eliminate password-based attack vectors inside remote desktops

checks.name: NAT Gateway for Outbound
checks.description: Subnets without public IPs should use NAT Gateway for deterministic outbound connectivity with static public IPs, avoiding default outbound access (which Azure is deprecating) and enabling firewall allow-listing

checks.name: Route Table on AVD Subnets
checks.description: User-Defined Routes (UDR) should be applied to AVD subnets to force outbound traffic through a central firewall or NVA for inspection, logging, and compliance with network segmentation policies

checks.name: Private Link for AVD Control Plane
checks.description: Azure Private Link for the AVD control plane routes feed, gateway, and broker traffic over private endpoints, ensuring session negotiation and metadata exchange never traverse the public internet

checks.name: Configuration Drift Detection
checks.description: For persistent/personal desktops or environments without golden image reimaging, drift detection should track unauthorized application installs, failed or missing Windows Updates, failed or missing application patches, and security baseline deviation. Use Intune compliance policies, SCCM, or Windows Update for Business reports to monitor patch levels, update failures, and configuration state over time

checks.name: IaC Deployment Used
checks.description: Host pools, session hosts, and supporting infrastructure should be deployed via Infrastructure as Code (ARM/Bicep/Terraform) for repeatability, version control, and consistent deployments across environments

checks.name: Session Host Update Strategy
checks.description: A documented session host update strategy should define when to patch-in-place vs. reimage/replace, maximum host age, rollback plans, and coordination with scaling plans to avoid capacity gaps during updates

checks.name: Load Balancing Algorithm
checks.description: Load balancing should use BreadthFirst during ramp-up (spreads users for warm-up) and DepthFirst during peak hours (fills hosts to maximize density). The algorithm should align with the scaling plan schedule

checks.name: Region Proximity
checks.description: Session hosts and their FSLogix profile storage should be in the same Azure region. Cross-region profile mounts add 50-200ms latency per file operation, severely degrading logon times and application performance

checks.name: OS Disk SSD Type
checks.description: Production session host OS disks should use SSD (Standard SSD minimum, Premium SSD recommended). HDD disks cause slow boot times, login storms, and poor application launch performance in multi-session scenarios

checks.name: Session Host Update Feature
checks.description: Session Host Update (preview) automates image rollout with health validation, rollback capability, and controlled batch sizing. Evaluate this feature to reduce manual effort for golden image lifecycle management

checks.name: App Group Assignment via Entra Groups
checks.description: Application group assignments should use Entra ID security groups rather than direct user assignment. Group-based assignment enables self-service via access packages, simplifies auditing, and scales with the org

checks.name: App Criticality Assessment
checks.description: Each application group should be rated for business criticality (1-5) to inform SLA targets, DR priority, scaling aggressiveness, and support response levels during incidents

checks.name: Sensitivity Labels on App Groups
checks.description: Microsoft Purview sensitivity labels should be applied to application groups to classify data sensitivity levels and enforce data loss prevention policies appropriate to each workload type

checks.name: Application Secrets in Key Vault
checks.description: Application credentials, API keys, and certificates should be stored in Azure Key Vault — never hardcoded in scripts, ARM templates, or FSLogix profile containers. Key Vault provides audited, RBAC-controlled access

checks.name: Multimedia Redirection
checks.description: Multimedia redirection offloads video rendering to the client device for smoother playback and reduced session host CPU usage. This is critical for browser-based video content and WebRTC calls on AVD

checks.name: Alerting Rules Configured
checks.description: Azure Monitor metric alerts should be configured for VM-level health (CPU >85%, available memory <1GB, disk queue length >2) and scaling failures to detect capacity issues before they impact users

checks.name: Performance Counters Configured
checks.description: Performance counters for CPU, memory, logical disk, network, and RemoteFX graphics should be configured in the Log Analytics data collection rule for session hosts to enable trend analysis and capacity planning

checks.name: Windows Event Logs Collected
checks.description: Windows event logs for FSLogix (Apps/Operational), TerminalServices-LocalSessionManager, and TerminalServices-RemoteConnectionManager should be forwarded to Log Analytics for session lifecycle troubleshooting

checks.name: Storage IOPS Monitoring
checks.description: FSLogix profile storage IOPS and throughput should be monitored against Azure Files/ANF tier limits. IOPS saturation during login storms causes profile mount timeouts and extended user logon times

checks.name: Defender for Cloud Enabled
checks.description: Microsoft Defender for Cloud (CSPM tier) should be enabled on the subscription to provide security posture assessment, vulnerability scanning, and regulatory compliance scoring for AVD resources

checks.name: SIEM Integration
checks.description: Microsoft Sentinel or an equivalent SIEM should be connected to the Log Analytics workspace for security event correlation, threat detection, incident response workflows, and AVD-specific analytics rules

checks.name: Compliance Reporting
checks.description: Monthly compliance reporting should track Windows security update installation rates, patch compliance percentage, and time-to-patch metrics across all session hosts to ensure vulnerability remediation SLAs are met

checks.name: Audit Log Collection
checks.description: Azure Activity logs, Entra ID audit/sign-in logs, and Key Vault diagnostic logs should be forwarded to Log Analytics to provide a complete audit trail for RBAC changes, authentication events, and secret access

checks.name: Resource Locks Applied
checks.description: CanNotDelete resource locks should be applied to critical AVD resources (host pools, workspaces, Log Analytics, profile storage) to prevent accidental deletion during cleanup operations or IaC redeployments

checks.name: Resource Group Separation
checks.description: AVD resources should be separated into functional resource groups: compute (session hosts), storage (profiles), networking (VNets/NSGs), and management (Log Analytics/vaults) — aligned with RBAC boundaries and lifecycle

checks.name: Control-Plane Recovery Plan
checks.description: A documented control-plane recovery plan should detail how to recreate host pools, app groups, workspaces, and scaling plans — preferably via IaC (Bicep/ARM/Terraform) for rapid, consistent redeployment

checks.name: Scaling Plan Active
checks.description: Scaling plans must be in Enabled state and not suspended. A suspended or disabled scaling plan means no automatic scale-out during peak demand and no scale-in during off-peak, causing either outages or cost waste

checks.name: Azure Security Baseline
checks.description: The AVD deployment should be evaluated against the Azure Security Benchmark baseline, which provides prescriptive security controls mapped to CIS, NIST, and PCI-DSS frameworks for cloud workloads

checks.name: Single-Region Concentration Risk
checks.description: If all AVD resources are deployed in a single Azure region, this concentration risk must be formally documented and accepted in the risk register, or mitigated with a multi-region failover strategy

checks.name: Multi-Region Host Pool
checks.description: A secondary host pool in a different Azure region should be provisioned (or ready to deploy) for disaster recovery. This host pool serves users when the primary region experiences a prolonged outage

checks.name: Active-Active/Passive Strategy
checks.description: BCDR strategy should be explicitly documented: active-active (both regions serve traffic), active-passive (standby region activates on failure), or no-recovery (accepted RPO of data loss). This drives architecture decisions

checks.name: Capacity Reservation for DR
checks.description: On-demand capacity reservations should be evaluated for the secondary/failover region to guarantee VM availability during regional outages when many customers may be competing for the same capacity

checks.name: VNet for Failover Region
checks.description: A virtual network in the failover region with on-premises connectivity (ExpressRoute/VPN) should be pre-established so that DR session hosts can reach domain controllers, file shares, and internal applications

checks.name: Platform vs Application LZ Separation
checks.description: AVD should be deployed in an application landing zone subscription, not in the platform subscription. Platform subscriptions host shared services (hub networking, DNS, firewall) consumed by workload subscriptions

checks.name: Enterprise Enrollment Model
checks.description: Tenant and subscription organization should follow CAF enterprise enrollment guidance — with clear EA/MCA hierarchy, department structure, and subscription vending aligned to workload teams and cost centers

checks.name: Shared Responsibility Documented
checks.description: A documented RACI matrix should define responsibilities between the platform team (networking, identity, policy) and the workload team (session hosts, images, FSLogix, app delivery, user support)

checks.name: Platform Connectivity Consumed
checks.description: AVD should consume centrally managed networking services (ExpressRoute/VPN gateways, Azure Firewall, DNS) from the platform connectivity subscription rather than deploying duplicate networking infrastructure

checks.name: Compliance Framework
checks.description: Relevant regulatory compliance framework (HIPAA, NIST 800-53, PCI-DSS, FedRAMP, ISO 27001) should be identified and Azure Policy initiative assignments configured to continuously assess compliance status

checks.name: Cloud Cache Configuration
checks.description: FSLogix Cloud Cache provides real-time profile replication across multiple storage providers for high availability. Evaluate whether profile HA requirements justify the added complexity and storage costs

checks.name: ODFC Container Separation
checks.description: Separating the Office Data (ODFC) container from the Profile container allows independent sizing, different storage tiers, and targeted backup strategies for Outlook data vs. general profile settings

checks.name: Profile Size Management
checks.description: Profile container size quotas should be configured (SizeInMBs registry value), cleanup policies for orphaned VHDs established, and container growth monitored to prevent storage exhaustion

checks.name: App Attach Storage Separated
checks.description: App Attach packages should be stored on a separate file share from FSLogix profiles to avoid IOPS contention during logon storms and allow independent scaling of application delivery storage

checks.name: FSLogix Version Current
checks.description: FSLogix should be updated to the latest release to receive bug fixes, performance improvements, and security patches. Check the FSLogix release notes for known issues with older versions

checks.name: Container Type VHDX
checks.description: VHDX format should be used instead of VHD for profile containers. VHDX supports dynamic expansion up to 1TB+, is more resilient to corruption, and provides better performance than legacy VHD format

checks.name: FlipFlopProfileDirectoryName
checks.description: FlipFlopProfileDirectoryName=1 names profile folders as username_SID instead of SID_username, making it significantly easier for admins to browse and troubleshoot individual user profiles on the file share

checks.name: DeleteLocalProfileWhenVHDShouldApply
checks.description: DeleteLocalProfileWhenVHDShouldApply=1 removes cached local profiles when an FSLogix container should be used, preventing data loss scenarios where users unknowingly save to a local profile that gets wiped on logoff

checks.name: Storage Permissions Configured
checks.description: NTFS and share-level permissions on the FSLogix file share must follow the documented permission model. Incorrect permissions cause profile mount failures, permission denied errors, or excessive access exposure

checks.name: BCDR Strategy for Profiles
checks.description: Profile BCDR strategy should be documented specifying whether no-recovery, Cloud Cache active-active, or manual failover is used. This determines RTO/RPO for user profile availability during storage outages

checks.name: HA Strategy for Profiles
checks.description: Profile high-availability should be implemented via ZRS storage (zone resilience) or Cloud Cache multi-provider configuration (cross-region resilience) based on availability SLA requirements

checks.name: AV Exclusions Complete (Full List)
checks.description: All recommended FSLogix antivirus exclusions (13+ paths including VHD/VHDX files, profile directories, and Cloud Cache directories) must be applied to prevent AV scanning from causing profile lock and corruption

checks.name: SMB Minimum Version
checks.description: Storage account SMB minimum version should be set to SMB 3.0+ to prevent downgrades to SMB 2.1, which lacks encryption and modern authentication, exposing profile data to network sniffing attacks

checks.name: SMB Channel Encryption
checks.description: SMB channel encryption should prefer AES-256-GCM for maximum security of profile data in transit. Weaker cipher suites (AES-128-CCM, AES-128-GCM) should be disabled where client support allows

checks.name: Kerberos Ticket Encryption
checks.description: Kerberos ticket encryption should use AES-256 only with RC4-HMAC disabled. RC4 is considered weak and susceptible to offline brute-force attacks for Kerberos ticket-granting ticket extraction

checks.name: Authentication Methods
checks.description: Storage account authentication should use Kerberos only, with NTLMv2 disabled. NTLM authentication exposes password hashes that can be captured and cracked or relayed in pass-the-hash attacks

checks.name: Storage Firewall Configured
checks.description: Storage account firewall default action should be set to Deny. Default Allow permits any Azure service or internet client to access the storage account, bypassing VNet and private endpoint isolation
checks.impact: Storage accounts with default Allow bypass network isolation — any Azure service or internet client can access profile data.

checks.name: Soft Delete Enabled
checks.description: Azure Files soft delete should be enabled with a retention period (7-365 days) to protect against accidental or malicious deletion of user profile containers, enabling self-service recovery

checks.name: Operations Baseline Established
checks.description: Azure server management services baseline (Azure Update Manager, Azure Monitor, Azure Automation, Defender for Cloud) should be established as the foundation for operational management of session hosts

checks.name: VM Token Expiration Risk
checks.description: Session hosts not reporting a heartbeat for >60 days may have expired registration tokens. This prevents the host from reconnecting after restart, effectively orphaning it from the host pool
checks.impact: Expired registration tokens prevent new session hosts from joining the host pool, blocking scale-out and replacement operations.

checks.name: Golden Image Update Process
checks.description: Golden image updates should follow a documented process with validation testing, canary deployment, staged rollout, and rollback criteria. Failed image updates should be detectable and reversible within the defined RTO

checks.name: Version Compliance
checks.description: AVD Agent, FSLogix, WebRTC redirector, and Multimedia Redirection extension versions should be tracked and updated to current releases. Outdated components miss bug fixes, performance improvements, and security patches

checks.name: Subnet IP Capacity
checks.description: AVD subnets should maintain at least 30% free IP addresses to accommodate scaling plan scale-out events. IP exhaustion prevents new session host deployment and blocks autoscale during peak demand
checks.impact: Subnets approaching IP exhaustion will prevent new session hosts from deploying, blocking scaling plans and causing outages during peak demand.

checks.name: AVD Required Outbound Rules
checks.description: When NSG default-deny outbound rules are configured, explicit allow rules for WindowsVirtualDesktop, AzureCloud, and AzureActiveDirectory service tags are required for session host registration and RDP connectivity
checks.impact: Missing AVD service tag outbound rules can cause session host registration failures, connection broker timeouts, and broken RDP sessions.

checks.name: VNet Peering Health
checks.description: All VNet peerings must be in Connected state. Disconnected or InitiatedPeering states break session host communication with profile storage, domain controllers, and centralized services in the hub VNet

checks.name: Ephemeral OS Disk (Pooled)
checks.description: Pooled session hosts should use ephemeral OS disks when possible. These are created on local VM storage, provide faster reimage/redeploy, eliminate storage costs, and ensure a clean state on every restart

checks.name: Disk Type Cost Optimization
checks.description: Premium SSD on pooled session hosts may be over-provisioned if ephemeral disks are not possible. Standard SSD often provides sufficient IOPS for non-persistent pooled hosts at significantly lower cost

checks.name: Session Host Health Status
checks.description: Session host health status should be monitored. Hosts reporting NeedsAssistance, Shutdown, or Unavailable may have domain join failures, agent crashes, or FSLogix mount errors requiring remediation

checks.name: RDP Drive Redirection
checks.description: Drive redirection should be restricted or disabled to prevent users from copying files between the remote session and local drives. This is a primary data exfiltration vector in regulated environments
checks.impact: Drive redirection allows users to copy sensitive data from sessions to local drives, creating a data exfiltration path.

checks.name: RDP Clipboard Redirection
checks.description: Clipboard redirection should be restricted or disabled in sensitive environments. Unrestricted clipboard allows copy-paste of text, images, and file paths between sessions and local devices
checks.impact: Clipboard redirection enables copy-paste of sensitive data between the remote session and local machine.

checks.name: RDP USB Redirection
checks.description: USB device redirection should be blocked by default unless specifically required for smart cards or hardware tokens. USB redirection can introduce malware or enable unauthorized data transfer via USB storage

checks.name: RDP Printer Redirection
checks.description: Printer redirection should be evaluated and restricted based on data classification. Unrestricted printing to local printers enables physical copies of sensitive data to leave secure environments

checks.name: RDP Security Summary
checks.description: Overall RDP device redirection security posture should be reviewed holistically across all redirection types (drives, clipboard, USB, printers, cameras, smart cards) to ensure alignment with data classification

checks.name: Guest Attestation Extension
checks.description: Guest Attestation extension should be installed on Trusted Launch VMs to enable boot integrity monitoring via Microsoft Defender for Cloud. This validates that Secure Boot and vTPM measurements are untampered

checks.name: Agent Version Currency
checks.description: AVD Agent version should be current across all session hosts. Agent version mismatches can cause inconsistent behavior, missed feature availability, and may lack fixes for known connection or registration issues

checks.name: Secure Boot Enabled
checks.description: Secure Boot should be enabled on all Trusted Launch VMs to ensure only signed, trusted bootloaders and OS kernels execute. This prevents boot-level malware (bootkits) from persisting across reboots
checks.impact: Without Secure Boot, VMs are vulnerable to bootkits and rootkits that load before the OS, potentially compromising the entire session host.

checks.name: vTPM Enabled
checks.description: Virtual TPM should be enabled on Trusted Launch VMs for measured boot attestation, BitLocker encryption, and Windows Hello for Business. vTPM validates boot integrity against known-good measurements
checks.impact: vTPM is required for BitLocker, measured boot attestation, and Windows Hello for Business. Without it, boot integrity cannot be verified.

checks.name: OS Disk Encryption
checks.description: OS disk encryption (Azure Disk Encryption, host-based encryption, or SSE with CMK) should be applied. Unencrypted disks expose credentials, cached tokens, and user data if a disk is detached or snapshotted
checks.impact: Unencrypted OS disks expose data at rest. If a disk is detached or snapshotted, all OS-level secrets and cached credentials are readable.

checks.name: Just-in-Time VM Access
checks.description: Just-in-Time VM access should be configured to keep management ports (RDP, SSH) closed by default and open them only on-demand with time-limited, audited, and approval-based access requests
checks.impact: Always-open management ports (RDP/SSH) dramatically increase brute-force attack surface. JIT reduces exposure to only when access is needed.

checks.name: Key Vault for Secrets
checks.description: Azure Key Vault should be present in AVD resource groups for managing certificates, connection strings, and deployment secrets. Key Vault provides RBAC-controlled, audited, soft-delete protected secret storage
checks.impact: Storing secrets in code, ARM templates, or storage accounts risks credential exposure. Key Vault provides audited, RBAC-controlled secret management.

checks.name: Private DNS Zone Linked
checks.description: Private DNS zone (privatelink.file.core.windows.net) must be created and linked to AVD VNets so private endpoint DNS resolution works correctly. Without this, clients resolve the public IP and bypass network isolation
checks.impact: Without private DNS zones linked to the VNet, private endpoint DNS resolution fails and traffic falls back to the public endpoint, bypassing network isolation.

checks.name: Network Watcher Enabled
checks.description: Network Watcher should be enabled in each Azure region hosting AVD resources to provide packet capture, NSG flow logs, connection troubleshooting, and topology visualization
checks.impact: Network Watcher provides packet capture, flow logs, and connection troubleshooting. Without it, diagnosing connectivity issues requires manual effort.

checks.name: Orphaned Disks Detected
checks.description: Unattached managed disks in AVD resource groups represent wasted storage costs and potential data exposure from decommissioned session hosts. These should be reviewed, snapshotted if needed, and deleted
checks.impact: Orphaned disks incur ongoing storage costs and may contain sensitive data from decommissioned session hosts.

checks.name: Orphaned NICs Detected
checks.description: Unattached NICs left from deleted session hosts may still have public IPs (incurring charges) and NSG associations that create governance blind spots. Clean up orphaned NICs after host decommissioning
checks.impact: Orphaned NICs with public IPs still incur charges and may retain NSG associations that create governance blind spots.

checks.name: Azure Policy Assignments
checks.description: Azure Policy assignments on AVD resource groups enforce guardrails — preventing non-compliant VM SKUs, requiring tags, enforcing encryption, and blocking public IPs — without relying on manual review
checks.impact: Without policy assignments, there is no guardrail preventing non-compliant resource deployments (wrong SKUs, missing tags, insecure configs).

checks.name: Alert Rules Configured
checks.description: At least one Azure Monitor alert rule should target AVD resources to detect host pool capacity issues, VM health degradation, storage throttling, or scaling plan failures before they impact users
checks.impact: Without alert rules, service degradation (high CPU, low disk, disconnected hosts) goes unnoticed until users report issues.

checks.name: Token Protection Policy
checks.description: Conditional Access token protection (token binding) binds access tokens to the originating device, preventing stolen token replay attacks that could grant unauthorized access to AVD sessions from another device
checks.impact: Without token binding, stolen access tokens can be replayed from any device, enabling lateral movement into AVD sessions.

checks.name: Image Version Freshness
checks.description: Azure Compute Gallery image versions used by session hosts should be less than 90 days old. Stale images mean newly deployed hosts start with unpatched baselines, increasing vulnerability exposure windows
checks.impact: Stale gallery images mean session hosts are deployed from unpatched base images, increasing vulnerability exposure until in-guest patching catches up.
checks.remediation: Update-AzGalleryImageVersion — create new image version via Azure Image Builder on a monthly cadence.

checks.name: OS Security Baselines
checks.description: Windows security baselines (Microsoft Security Baselines or CIS Benchmarks) should be applied to session hosts via Intune or GPO for consistent hardening of OS settings, audit policies, and attack surface reduction
checks.impact: Unmanaged OS configurations leave session hosts vulnerable to local exploitation, unauthorized configuration changes, and malware.

checks.name: VM Quota Headroom
checks.description: vCPU quota usage should be below 80% in each Azure region to provide headroom for scaling plan scale-out events. Near-limit quota causes VM allocation failures during peak demand surges
checks.impact: If vCPU quota is near the limit, scaling plans cannot start additional session hosts during peak demand, causing user connection failures.
checks.remediation: Request quota increase via Azure Portal > Subscriptions > Usage + quotas, or use New-AzSupportTicket.

checks.name: Capacity Reservation
checks.description: Capacity Reservation Groups guarantee VM availability for mission-critical host pools during regional capacity events. Without reservations, allocation failures may block scaling during Azure-wide demand spikes
checks.impact: Without capacity reservations, VM allocation failures during regional capacity events can prevent scaling plan from starting hosts.

checks.name: Budget Alerts Configured
checks.description: Azure Cost Management budgets with alert thresholds (80%/100%/120%) should be configured on AVD subscriptions or resource groups to detect unexpected spend before it impacts the organization's cloud budget
checks.impact: Without budgets and alerts, unexpected cost spikes from misconfigured scaling plans, orphaned resources, or overprovisioning go undetected.

checks.name: Reserved Instance Coverage
checks.description: Azure Reserved Instances (1 or 3 year) or Savings Plans should be evaluated for always-on VMs in personal host pools. RI pricing offers 40-72% savings compared to pay-as-you-go for predictable, steady-state workloads
checks.impact: Pay-as-you-go pricing for always-on VMs costs 40-72% more than equivalent Reserved Instances over a 1-3 year term.

checks.name: Cross-Resource Tag Compliance
checks.description: All AVD resources (VMs, storage accounts, VNets, NSGs, host pools) should have consistent tags applied. Inconsistent tagging breaks cost allocation reports, automation runbooks, and governance policy evaluation
checks.impact: Inconsistent tagging prevents accurate cost allocation, automated governance, and makes it difficult to identify resource ownership during incidents.
checks.remediation: Use Azure Policy 'Require a tag and its value' to enforce mandatory tags across resource groups.

checks.name: Hub Firewall Present
checks.description: Azure Firewall or network virtual appliance should exist in the hub network for centralized egress filtering
checks.impact: Without a centralized firewall, egress traffic from session hosts is unfiltered, increasing risk of data exfiltration and C2 communication.

checks.name: VPN or ExpressRoute Gateway
checks.description: Hub network should have a VPN or ExpressRoute gateway for hybrid connectivity to on-premises resources
checks.impact: Without a gateway, hybrid identity (AD DS), file shares, and line-of-business apps on-premises are unreachable from AVD session hosts.

checks.name: Diagnostic Log Categories
checks.description: All recommended AVD diagnostic log categories (Checkpoint, Error, Management, Connection, HostRegistration, AgentHealthStatus) should be enabled
checks.impact: Missing log categories create blind spots — e.g., without Connection logs, you cannot troubleshoot session failures in AVD Insights.
checks.remediation: Update diagnostic setting to enable all 6 recommended categories: Checkpoint, Error, Management, Connection, HostRegistration, AgentHealthStatus.

checks.name: Key Vault Private Endpoint
checks.description: Azure Key Vaults in AVD resource groups should use private endpoints to prevent secret exposure over public networks
checks.impact: Key Vaults without private endpoints expose secret retrieval traffic to the public internet, increasing risk of credential interception.
checks.remediation: New-AzPrivateEndpoint for Key Vault with 'vault' group ID, then configure private DNS zone privatelink.vaultcore.azure.net.

checks.name: Host Pool Private Link
checks.description: AVD host pools should use Private Link for control-plane traffic to keep session brokering off the public internet
checks.impact: Without Private Link, AVD control-plane traffic (session brokering, diagnostics) traverses the public internet, increasing attack surface.
